intro: Sahulia suboppositifolia, also known as Bhumyamalaki, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Sahulia suboppositifolia include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, making it useful in treating various liver and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sahulia suboppositifolia include decoctions, infusions, and churnas (Ayurvedic powder preparations), which are used to promote liver health and alleviate digestive discomfort.
